summary: SKFont.ContainsGlyphs returns false for emoji strings containing Unicode variation selectors (e.g. ❤️) on Android because the variation selector character U+FE0F maps to glyph 0.

screenshots.description: Screenshot showing fallback font debug info

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: ContainsGlyphs implementation in SKFont.cs uses Array.IndexOf(glyphs, 0) == -1, which returns false whenever any glyph maps to 0. Variation selectors (U+FE0F) have no standalone glyph and map to glyph ID 0 on Android, causing the entire check to fail.

regression.reason: Reporter states it worked in 2.88.9 but fails in 3.116.0. The ContainsGlyphs API may have changed behavior between versions.

analysis.summary: ContainsGlyphs(string) fails for emoji strings containing variation selectors on Android. The ❤️ emoji is two UTF-16 characters (U+2764 + U+FE0F). GetGlyphs maps each char to a glyph ID; the variation selector U+FE0F returns glyph ID 0 (not found). ContainsGlyphs then returns false because it checks Array.IndexOf(glyphs, 0) == -1. The fix should skip variation selectors and other combining/modifier code points when checking for glyph presence.

codeInvestigation.finding: ContainsGlyphs(string) delegates to GetGlyphs(string) then private ContainsGlyphs(ushort[]). The private method returns Array.IndexOf(glyphs, (ushort)0) == -1, meaning ALL glyphs must be non-zero for the method to return true.

GetGlyphs(ReadOnlySpan<char>) calls sk_font_text_to_glyphs with UTF-16 encoding. For the string '❤️' (2 chars), this produces 2 glyph IDs. Unicode variation selector U+FE0F is not a visible character and most fonts map it to glyph 0.

keySignals.text: font.ContainsGlyphs(text.substring(0,1)) returns true. font.ContainsGlyphs(text.substring(1,1)) returns false.
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms the heart character (index 0) has a glyph but the variation selector (index 1) does not, causing the combined check to fail.
The fallback font is found but its ContainsGlyphs returns false due to the variation selector issue, so the character is not rendered.

keySignals.text: On iOS all work and the fallbackfont is appleEmoji
keySignals.interpretation: Apple Emoji font likely handles the variation sequence as a single cluster and returns a valid glyph for the full sequence, whereas Android fonts do not.

analysis.rationale: This is a type/bug because ContainsGlyphs incorrectly returns false for emoji strings containing Unicode variation selectors. The root cause is in the private ContainsGlyphs(ushort[]) method which treats any glyph ID 0 as a failure, but variation selectors legitimately map to glyph 0 while the base character has a valid glyph. Classified as os/Android because iOS works correctly. Medium severity because it affects emoji rendering with a workaround available (pass only the base codepoint).
workarounds: Use char.IsHighSurrogate/IsLowSurrogate checks and pass only the base emoji codepoint (without the variation selector) to ContainsGlyphs.

Convert the string to Unicode scalar values using StringInfo or Rune enumeration, then call ContainsGlyphs(ReadOnlySpan<int> codepoints) with individual codepoints, skipping variation selectors (U+FE0F, U+FE0E).
nextQuestions: Does SKFontManager.MatchCharacter handle variation sequences correctly on Android?
What was the ContainsGlyphs implementation in 2.88.9 that made it work correctly?

proposals.title: Workaround: use Rune enumeration to skip variation selectors
proposals.description: Enumerate runes and filter out combining/variation selectors before calling ContainsGlyphs.

proposals.title: Fix: ContainsGlyphs should check each base grapheme cluster independently
proposals.description: The private ContainsGlyphs(ushort[]) should be changed to check glyphs corresponding to base codepoints only, skipping variation selectors and other zero-width combiners. Alternatively, use the ReadOnlySpan<int> codepoints overload internally.

resolution.recommendedReason: The workaround requires users to understand Unicode variation selectors; a fix in the library is more correct and backward-compatible.

actionability.reason: Regression with partial repro. Root cause is identified in ContainsGlyphs logic but a reliable minimal repro and confirmation of version difference is needed before fixing.

missingInfo: Minimal self-contained code sample demonstrating the issue
Confirmation of which version it first broke in (3.116.0 vs earlier 3.x versions)

actions.description: Explain the variation selector root cause and provide workaround
